channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Sony increasing the price of PlayStation Plus for new customers
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

May 19, 2026

1 Min Read

Sony is raising the price of PlayStation Plus for new customers in key regions including the UK, Europe, and United States.

The new pricing structure will be rolled out on May 20, 2026, and will only apply to new subscribers in all markets—excluding Turkey and India, where current subscribers will be asked to pay more. 

Sony said the move is a response to "ongoing market conditions." It comes months after the company increased the price of console hardware around the globe. 

"Starting May 20, PlayStation Plus prices for new customers will increase in select regions. Due to ongoing market conditions, prices will start at $10.99 USD / €9.99 EUR / £7.99 GBP for 1-month subscriptions and $27.99 USD / €27.99 EUR / £21.99 GBP for 3-month subscriptions," reads a statement from the official PlayStation account on X. 

"This price change does not apply to current subscribers (except in Turkey and India) unless the existing subscription changes or lapses." 

Related:Arc Raiders tops 16 million sales and could be heading to China

The increase will effectively require new entry-level subscribers to pay $1/£1 more per month than existing users.

Right now, a PlayStation Plus Essential subscription (the base tier of the service) costs $9.99 / £6.99 per-month in the United States and United Kingdom, respectively. That price will increase to $10.99 / £7.99 per month later this week. 

The three-month Essential plan will also receive a similar price hike. It's unclear how the other PlayStation Plus tiers—Premium and Extra—are impacted, but it's worth remembering that an Essential subscription is required to play online. 

Game Developer has reached out to Sony for more information.

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton.
